message:
perf(btree): fast-path CellSlotCache::insert for MRU front entry

Binary-search on a single page repeatedly hits the same MRU entry in
the cell-slot LRU. The previous insert() remove(0) + insert(0, entry) +
truncate sequence paid O(CELL_SLOT_CACHE_ENTRIES) memmoves on the entries
Vec every call, shuffling the front entry back to the front. Mutate in
place when the target is already at entries[0], leaving the slow LRU
promote for genuine non-front inserts.

Before: 240 ns / insert (10M ops)
After:    4 ns / insert (10M ops) — 60.25x speedup
Bench: `cargo test -p fsqlite-btree --release --lib
       bench_cell_slot_cache_insert_front_entry -- --ignored --nocapture`
       (prefill=32 entries, iters=5_000_000)
Parity: test_cell_slot_cache_invalidation_tracks_page_image_mutation +
        prop_cell_slot_cache_matches_fresh_parse_after_random_mutations
        pass; 363/364 btree lib tests pass (the one failure,
        prop_index_btree_structural_invariants_hold_after_random_mutations,
        is pre-existing on clean HEAD and unrelated to this change).
Bead: bd-4i4vh

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-4br8p","title":"WASM 6.6: Concurrent writer benchmark suite (browser-based perf comparison)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:24:49.568672685Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T04:25:38.098625006Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4br8p","depends_on_id":"bd-1zz3b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4br8p","depends_on_id":"bd-3vrea","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1612,"issue_id":"bd-4br8p","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Task 6.6: Concurrent Writer Benchmark Suite

## What
Browser-based benchmark comparing FrankenSQLite concurrent writers against sql.js, wa-sqlite, and official sqlite-wasm.

## Benchmark Scenarios
1. N writers inserting into different tables (no conflict)
2. N writers inserting into same table (page contention)
3. Mixed read/write workload (OLTP simulation)
4. Single writer throughput (baseline comparison)

## Expected Results
For scenario 1 (no conflict):
- FrankenSQLite (concurrent): ~Nx throughput improvement
- All others: ~1x (serialized)

## Deliverable
Interactive web page at `/benchmarks/` showing results as charts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Benchmarks run in browser and report results
- Fair comparison (same SQL workload)
- Charts show throughput (ops/sec) and latency (p50/p95/p99)
","created_at":"2026-03-02T04:25:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4cldv","title":"Track K: UPDATE/DELETE Cursor Reuse — Eliminate Re-Seek Per Modification","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:28:00.141767460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T19:57:46.513877852Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4cldv","depends_on_id":"bd-mn8oa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:51:15.908106673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-4cldv","depends_on_id":"bd-yywuv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-31T19:51:16.499087983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3378,"issue_id":"bd-4cldv","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Problem
UPDATE and DELETE are 6-8x slower than C SQLite because each modified row requires a full B-tree seek to find it. For batch updates (UPDATE SET x=y WHERE id IN (1,2,3,...)), each row is sought independently.

## Solution: Cursor-Based Scan-and-Modify
Instead of seek-modify-seek-modify for each row, use a single cursor scan:
1. Open cursor at first target row
2. Modify in place (or mark for deletion)
3. Advance cursor to next target row (often the next cell on same leaf!)
4. Repeat without re-seeking from root

For WHERE clauses that produce sequential rowids (the common case for batch UPDATE/DELETE), this turns N × O(log n) seeks into N × O(1) cursor advances.

## Alien Graveyard References:
- §5.7 Cursor Stability (Gray et al.) — hold cursor across modifications
- §4.8 Batch Modification Operators — modify without re-seeking

## Expected Impact: UPDATE from 7.86x slower to ~2x slower, DELETE from 5.60x slower to ~2x slower
","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:28:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4eue","title":"§20 Key Reference Files (C Source + Asupersync + Project Docs)","description":"## SUMMARY
Enumerates the key reference files (§20) that FrankenSQLite development must consult, organized into three categories: C SQLite source files (for spec extraction only), asupersync modules (for integration), and project documents (for implementation guidance). The C SQLite source table lists 15 files (sqliteInt.h, btree.c, pager.c, wal.c, vdbe.c, select.c, where.c, wherecode.c, whereexpr.c, whereInt.h, parse.y, tokenize.c, func.c, expr.c, build.c) with their purposes, approximate line counts, and what to extract from each. The asupersync modules table lists 11 integration points (raptorq, sync, mpsc channel, oneshot, cx, lab/runtime, lab/explorer, eprocess, oracle/eprocess, conformal, database/sqlite). The project documents table lists 6 documents with their purposes and when to consult them, establishing COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md as the superseding source of truth.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- **C SQLite Source (15 files):**
  - sqliteInt.h (5882 lines): All struct definitions (Btree, BtCursor, Pager, Wal, Vdbe, Mem, Table, Index, Column, Expr, Select), all #define constants, all function prototypes. The Rosetta Stone.
  - btree.c (11568 lines): Page format, cell format, cursor movement, insert/delete with rebalancing, overflow pages, freelist. Focus on balance_nonroot (~800 lines).
  - pager.c (7834 lines): Pager state machine (OPEN, READER, WRITER_LOCKED, WRITER_CACHEMOD, WRITER_DBMOD, WRITER_FINISHED, ERROR), journal format, hot journal detection, cache eviction.
  - wal.c (4621 lines): WAL header/frame format, checksum algorithm, wal-index hash table, checkpoint algorithm, WAL_WRITE_LOCK (replaced by MVCC).
  - vdbe.c (9316 lines): Giant switch statement for all opcodes. Authoritative opcode behavior.
  - select.c (8972 lines): SELECT compilation to VDBE: result columns, FROM flattening, subqueries, compound, DISTINCT, ORDER BY, LIMIT.
  - where.c (7858 lines): Index selection, cost estimation, OR optimization, skip-scan, automatic index. WhereTerm/WhereLoop/WherePath structures.
  - wherecode.c (2936 lines): WHERE codegen (WhereLoop to VDBE opcodes).
  - whereexpr.c (1943 lines): WHERE expression analysis feeding optimizer/codegen.
  - whereInt.h (668 lines): WHERE internal structs/flags/macros.
  - parse.y (2160 lines): LEMON grammar. Authoritative SQL grammar.
  - tokenize.c (899 lines): Token types, keywords, literal parsing, comments.
  - func.c (3461 lines): All scalar and aggregate functions with edge cases.
  - expr.c (7702 lines): Expression compilation, affinity, collation, constant folding.
  - build.c (5815 lines): DDL processing, schema modification, type affinity from type names.
- **Asupersync Modules (11 integration points):** raptorq (RFC 6330 codec), sync (Mutex/RwLock/Condvar), mpsc (two-phase MPSC channel), oneshot (commit response delivery), cx (capability context), lab/runtime (deterministic runtime), lab/explorer (DPOR + Mazurkiewicz traces), eprocess (anytime-valid monitoring), oracle/eprocess (test harness + certificates), conformal (distribution-free stats), database/sqlite (API reference).
- **Project Documents (6 docs):** This spec (source of truth), EXISTING_SQLITE_STRUCTURE.md (C SQLite behavior), docs/rfc6330.txt (RaptorQ), AGENTS.md (coding guidelines), MVCC_SPECIFICATION.md (legacy, superseded by §5), PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md (legacy, superseded by §8).

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
- NI-1: Line numbers in C SQLite source are approximate and vary by version. Do NOT rely on line numbers. Use function/struct names and spec invariants as source of truth.
- NI-2: COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md supersedes all other documents (MVCC_SPECIFICATION.md, PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md are historical reference only).
- NI-3: Implementation MUST consult EXISTING_SQLITE_STRUCTURE.md for C SQLite behavior, not C source directly.
- NI-4: AGENTS.md coding guidelines MUST be reviewed before every coding session.
- NI-5: FrankenSQLite's public API mirrors asupersync's SQLite wrapper API (src/database/sqlite.rs) for familiarity.
- NI-6: All asupersync integration points listed MUST be used via their documented APIs (not reimplemented).

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
1. `test_reference_doc_exists` - Verify EXISTING_SQLITE_STRUCTURE.md exists and is non-empty at expected path.
2. `test_spec_doc_exists` - Verify COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md exists at expected path.
3. `test_agents_md_exists` - Verify AGENTS.md exists at expected path.
4. `test_asupersync_raptorq_importable` - Verify asupersync raptorq module is importable/linkable.
5. `test_asupersync_cx_importable` - Verify asupersync cx (capability context) module is importable/linkable.
6. `test_asupersync_lab_runtime_importable` - Verify asupersync lab/runtime module is importable/linkable.
7. `test_asupersync_mpsc_importable` - Verify asupersync channel/mpsc module is importable/linkable.
8. `test_asupersync_eprocess_importable` - Verify asupersync eprocess module is importable/linkable.
9. `test_api_mirrors_asupersync_sqlite` - Verify FrankenSQLite public API surface matches asupersync database/sqlite.rs wrapper shape.
10. `test_no_direct_csqlite_source_imports` - Grep codebase for direct C SQLite source references; assert none found in implementation code (only in spec extraction docs).

- test_e2e_bd_4eue_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
Build verification test that validates the reference file manifest:
- Assert all 15 C SQLite source files are documented in the spec with purpose and extraction guidance.
- Assert all 11 asupersync modules are listed with integration rationale.
- Assert all 6 project documents exist at their expected paths.
- Assert COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md is marked as superseding MVCC_SPECIFICATION.md and PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Assert FrankenSQLite public API compiles and links against asupersync dependencies.
- Log: (file_name, exists, purpose_documented, integration_verified).

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- AC-1: All reference files documented in §20 are accounted for in the project structure.
- AC-2: Asupersync integration points compile and link correctly.
- AC-3: No implementation code directly references C SQLite source (only spec extraction documents do).
- AC-4: AGENTS.md review gate enforced in development workflow.
- AC-5: Public API mirrors asupersync's SQLite wrapper API shape.","notes":"2026-02-09 IndigoBear: added crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_4eue_reference_index_compliance.rs and e2e/reference_index_audit.sh; cleaned direct C-source filename mentions in parser/vfs/types/wal/btree comments. Validation: reference_index_audit.sh passes and regex scan over non-harness crates for C-source filename tokens is clean. Full cargo test rerun for bd_4eue harness test is pending due persistent shared cargo lock; isolated CARGO_TARGET_DIR recompilation fails in external /dp/asupersync with pre-existing Rust 2024 keyword ('gen') errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:01.464128458Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:21:55.828944019Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:21:55.828917679Z","close_reason":"Implemented reference-index audit + compliance tests and validated with passing bead test/e2e script","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4eue","depends_on_id":"bd-1qb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1613,"issue_id":"bd-4eue","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §20 Key Reference Files

### C SQLite Source (spec extraction only)
Line numbers approximate, vary by version. Use function/struct names as source of truth.

| File | Purpose | Key Contents |
|------|---------|-------------|
| sqliteInt.h (~5,882 LOC) | Main internal header | All struct defs (Btree, BtCursor, Pager, Wal, Vdbe, Mem, Table, Index, Column, Expr, Select), all #define constants. Rosetta Stone. |
| btree.c (~11,568) | B-tree engine | Page format, cell format, cursor movement, insert/delete with rebalancing, overflow, freelist. balance_nonroot ~800 lines (8230-9033). |
| pager.c (~7,834) | Page cache | Pager state machine (OPEN→READER→WRITER_*→ERROR), journal format, hot journal detection, cache eviction. |
| wal.c (~4,621) | WAL subsystem | WAL header/frame format, checksum algorithm, wal-index hash table, checkpoint, WAL_WRITE_LOCK (replaced by MVCC). |
| vdbe.c (~9,316) | VDBE interpreter | Giant switch for all opcodes. Authoritative opcode semantics. |
| select.c (~8,972) | SELECT compilation | Result columns, FROM flattening, subquery, compound, DISTINCT, ORDER BY, LIMIT. |
| where.c (~7,858) | WHERE optimization | Index selection, cost estimation, OR optimization, skip-scan. WhereTerm/WhereLoop/WherePath. |
| wherecode.c (~2,936) | WHERE codegen | WhereLoop → VDBE opcodes. |
| whereexpr.c (~1,943) | WHERE expression analysis | Term handling feeding optimizer/codegen. |
| whereInt.h (~668) | WHERE internals | Internal structs, flags, macros. |
| parse.y (~2,160) | LEMON grammar | Authoritative SQL grammar. |
| tokenize.c (~899) | SQL tokenizer | Token types, keywords, literals, comments. |
| func.c (~3,461) | Built-in functions | All scalar/aggregate implementations + edge cases. |
| expr.c (~7,702) | Expression handling | Compilation, affinity, collation, constant folding. |
| build.c (~5,815) | DDL processing | CREATE/DROP/ALTER compilation, schema modification. |

### Asupersync Modules
raptorq/ (RFC 6330 codec), sync/ (Mutex/RwLock/Condvar), channel/mpsc (2-phase MPSC), channel/oneshot, cx/ (capability context), lab/runtime (deterministic), lab/explorer (DPOR + Mazurkiewicz), obligation/eprocess, lab/oracle/eprocess, lab/conformal, database/sqlite (API reference).

### Project Documents
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC (source of truth, always consult). EXISTING_SQLITE_STRUCTURE.md (C behavior). docs/rfc6330.txt (RaptorQ). AGENTS.md (coding guidelines). MVCC_SPECIFICATION.md (legacy, superseded by §5). PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md (legacy, superseded by §8).
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:01Z"},{"id":1614,"issue_id":"bd-4eue","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Tests / Automated Checks Required

1. **test_reference_paths_exist_in_repo**: For any referenced in-repo file/dir (e.g., `legacy_sqlite_code/`, docs files), assert path exists.
2. **test_reference_table_has_no_duplicates**: Ensure no duplicate file entries and that table rows are unique.
3. **test_reference_index_covers_all_major_subsystems**: Ensure there is at least one reference entry for: B-tree, pager, wal, vdbe, parser, planner, functions.

## E2E Script

- **e2e/reference_index_audit.sh** (planned):
  - Print the resolved absolute paths (repo-local and /dp-local).
  - For missing /dp paths (asupersync), emit a clear warning but do not hard-fail unless required by CI.
  - Emit a machine-readable JSON report for diagnostics.

## Logging Requirements

- Audit script logs structured records: `ref_kind` (c_sqlite|asupersync|doc), `path`, `exists`, `notes`.
- If a key reference is missing, emit ERROR with a hint about how to obtain it.

## Acceptance Criteria

- The reference index can be audited automatically and stays accurate over time.
- Missing references are immediately discoverable via a deterministic audit report.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:30:51Z"},{"id":1615,"issue_id":"bd-4eue","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## E2E Test (Named)

- prop_bd_4eue_structure_compliance: property check that required structure blocks are present.

- test_e2e_bd_4eue_compliance: run end-to-end scenario proving this bead's path with deterministic artifact capture.

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: emit stage-level progress and key branch decisions for this bead scope.
- INFO: emit summary counters and completion status for this bead scope.
- WARN: emit degraded-mode or retry conditions encountered during this bead scope.
- ERROR: emit terminal failure diagnostics with reproducible context for this bead scope.
- Reference: bd-1fpm structured repro-bundle logging conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test identifiers for this bead are concrete and executable.
- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:16Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-4i4vh","title":"PERF-btree: eliminate O(N) LRU shuffle in CellSlotCache::insert front-entry hot path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T02:55:45.026618602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:17:48.835277606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:17:48.834866547Z","close_reason":"Front-entry fast path in CellSlotCache::insert: 60.25x speedup (240ns → 4ns per insert on the hot binary-search pattern) via microbench bench_cell_slot_cache_insert_front_entry. Eliminates O(N) entries-Vec remove+insert shuffle when target entry is already MRU. Slow path retained as insert_slow() for bench comparison and rare non-front hits.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4ps9d","title":"Hint bits on PageVersion: cache committed status to skip CommitIndex lookups (PostgreSQL HEAP_XMIN_COMMITTED pattern)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:25:44.535895257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-19T00:39:25.430395408Z","closed_at":"2026-03-19T00:39:25.429727959Z","close_reason":"CommitIndex fast array already provides O(1) atomic committed-status lookup for small page numbers. This is equivalent to PostgreSQL hint bits. Additional per-PageVersion caching would save only one atomic load (~2ns) - not worth the complexity.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2474,"issue_id":"bd-4ps9d","author":"ubuntu","text":"Background: PostgreSQL sets HEAP_XMIN_COMMITTED hint bits directly in tuple headers. First reader that discovers a txn committed sets the bit; all subsequent readers skip CLOG lookup. FrankenSQLite traverses CommitIndex on every page version access to check visibility. Fix: Add committed: bool flag (or AtomicBool for concurrent access) to PageVersion in VersionArena. On first visibility check that confirms committed, set the flag. Subsequent checks return immediately. Files: core_types.rs (PageVersion add committed flag), lifecycle.rs (set flag on commit confirmation), ssi_validation.rs (check flag before CommitIndex lookup). Tests: hint_bit_set_on_first_read_after_commit, hint_bit_skips_commit_index, hint_bit_concurrent_readers_safe, benchmark_hint_bit_vs_commit_index_lookup. Success: page version committed status cached after first check, measurable reduction in CommitIndex contention.
","created_at":"2026-03-18T23:49:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4uvi","title":"Fixtures: Implement discovery + selection (scan /dp for SQLite DBs safely)","description":"## Goal
Build a safe fixture discovery mechanism that can identify candidate SQLite DB files under `/dp`.

## Requirements
- Use `find -L /dp ...`-style traversal (follow symlink) but with:
  - bounded depth
  - allowlist roots (optional)
  - denylist patterns (e.g. `node_modules`, huge caches)
- Identify likely SQLite DBs by extension and by magic header check ("SQLite format 3\0").
- Never modify source files.

## Output
- Candidate list with:
  - absolute path
  - file size
  - header ok?
  - classification tags ("beads", "cache", "sample", etc.) based on path heuristics

## Acceptance
- Running the discovery tool yields a list that includes:
  - multiple `/dp/**/.beads/beads.db`
  - known sample DBs (e.g. northwind)
- Discovery can be restricted (flags) to keep runtime reasonable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:04.179917870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:28:30.938186675Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:28:30.938164353Z","close_reason":"Implemented fixture_discovery module in fsqlite-harness: DiscoveryConfig, Candidate, discover_sqlite_files(), check_sqlite_header(), classify_path(), human_size(). 8 tests passing including /dp integration test. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fixtures","tooling"],"comments":[{"id":1617,"issue_id":"bd-4uvi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implements fixture discovery that scans /dp for SQLite database files
- Selection logic filters and validates discovered files safely (read-only access)
- Returns a list of usable fixture paths for differential testing
- Handles missing directories and permission errors gracefully","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4x6i","title":"frankentui Dashboard: Guided corruption -> repair storyline view","description":"## Goal
Add a guided demo mode in the dashboard that runs the durability story and displays steps as they happen.

## Steps UI
- encode sidecar
- corrupt file
- sqlite fails (show error)
- repair progress
- final integrity + sha match

## Acceptance

- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:26:02Z"}]}
@@ -1694,6 +1695,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.8","title":"I8: add concurrency-aware slack, asymmetric right-edge split policy, pre-split threshold, dynamic fill factor, and deferred cleanup","description":"## Goal
Keep inserts leaf-local longer by changing the physical split policy rather than just coordinating around the old one.

## Scope
- lower occupancy targets where concurrency pressure makes compactness harmful
- use asymmetric right-edge splits so the fresh right-most page gets the slack in append-like workloads
- add a right-edge pre-split threshold that acts before the hottest leaf is fully saturated
- allow contention-adaptive fill factors for hot tables/regions when retry or parent-version-change rates rise
- support private would-be sibling or phantom-leaf staging for multi-insert transactions when that safely amortizes structural work
- permit temporary structural untidiness with deferred/background cleanup rather than forcing compactness on the hot concurrent path

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- focused contention rows show lower broad-rebalance frequency and lower right-edge or parent-related retry pressure
- unit/property tests cover asymmetric split correctness, phantom-leaf or private-sibling staging safety where used, deferred-cleanup safety, and contention-adaptive thresholds
- structured logs expose when the engine enters adaptive low-fill, append-aware pre-split, phantom-leaf staging, or deferred-cleanup behavior
- append-heavy rows stay leaf-local longer without creating hidden correctness debt or unbounded cleanup backlog","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- focused contention rows show lower broad-rebalance frequency and lower right-edge or parent-related retry pressure
- unit/property tests cover asymmetric split correctness, phantom-leaf or private-sibling staging safety where used, deferred-cleanup safety, and contention-adaptive thresholds
- structured logs expose when the engine enters adaptive low-fill, append-aware pre-split, phantom-leaf staging, or deferred-cleanup behavior
- append-heavy rows stay leaf-local longer without creating hidden correctness debt or unbounded cleanup backlog","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit/property tests for asymmetric right-edge splits, adaptive fill-factor changes, phantom-leaf/private-sibling staging safety, deferred cleanup safety, and threshold hysteresis or stability behavior
- make the split policy explicit about occupancy shape: internal pages may remain intentionally sparser than leaves, and hot/right-edge leaves may reserve extra slack so the new right-most page starts with room while the old page stays relatively fuller
- add focused e2e runs on append-like and non-append-like rows so the policy is not validated only on one geometry
- emit structured log events or counters for right_edge_presplit, adaptive_fill_factor_state, phantom_leaf_stage_used, deferred_cleanup_backlog, split_policy_choice, and deferred_cleanup_checkpoint_repair
- keep deferred cleanup explicitly bounded and observable so temporary structural untidiness improves concurrency without becoming permanent hidden debt or invisible checkpoint-era work","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:54.507814053Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:11.210888917Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adaptive-control","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","right-edge","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:54.507814053Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:38.081221818Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:13:58.906253068Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2292,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.8","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:

 {"id":"bd-db300.9.9","title":"I9: design version-gated parent micro-publish with local structural restart","description":"## Goal
Specify the first-wave optimistic structural protocol that replaces the already-falsified preclaim family.

## Scope
- define a protocol that reads a parent version token, computes split/sibling images privately, acquires the parent only for the final publish step, verifies the version, and releases immediately
- specify the parent version token or generation mechanism readable without long-lived exclusive ownership
- define local restart rules on mismatch so the whole transaction is not discarded unless semantically necessary
- spell out correctness, crash, and recovery constraints

## Acceptance Criteria
Success means:
- the protocol is specific enough to implement without reopening the markdown plan
- the design clearly states version-token semantics, publish-window boundaries, mismatch handling, and rollback/recovery expectations
- the design includes a step-by-step publish/restart sequence plus invariants that must hold before and after publish
- the design explicitly documents why it does not regress into longer shared ownership or universal waiting","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the protocol is specific enough to implement without reopening the markdown plan
- the design clearly states version-token semantics, publish-window boundaries, mismatch handling, and rollback/recovery expectations
- the design includes a step-by-step publish/restart sequence plus invariants that must hold before and after publish
- the design explicitly documents why it does not regress into longer shared ownership or universal waiting","notes":"Design-packet requirement:
- include a concrete sequence description for prepare, validate, publish, mismatch, and local-restart flows
- name the version anchors or generation counters involved, the exact point where shared ownership begins/ends, and the evidence hooks that later implementation/verification beads must expose
- explicitly compare the protocol against the rejected preclaim family so future agents do not reintroduce the same bad idea under new names
- state explicitly that any waiting policy here is narrow and structural-only for short publish windows; this bead must not become a Trojan horse for universal blocking waits on ordinary page conflicts
- record the local-restart boundary in enough detail that future agents can tell when restart stays local versus when true semantic invalidation forces wider restart","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:04.763900339Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:52:11.767502739Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","design","micro-publish","mvcc","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:04.763900339Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:09.437778928Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:59.260884089Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:21:48.547656496Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:19.841786719Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2293,"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.9","author":"ubuntu","text":"Clause coverage:

 {"id":"bd-dcjy","title":"Fast smoke test: 30-second infrastructure verification before full E2E","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:59:29.216401242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T08:39:17.702453263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T08:39:17.702434738Z","close_reason":"Implemented 7-check fast smoke test module with 8 passing tests, all in <1s","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","depends_on_id":"bd-1opl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","depends_on_id":"bd-1q00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","depends_on_id":"bd-2bdw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","depends_on_id":"bd-2e3s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","depends_on_id":"bd-2zkh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","depends_on_id":"bd-3cbw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T21:54:24Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1737,"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Fast Smoke Test: 30-Second Infrastructure Verification Before Full E2E

### Why This Is Critical
The full E2E suite takes 5-10+ minutes. Before running it, a 30-second smoke test verifies that all infrastructure is working: golden copies exist, both backends can open databases, the workload generator works, corruption injection works. This catches configuration issues immediately instead of 5 minutes into a run.

### Subcommand: `e2e_runner run-smoke`

### Checks (must all pass in <30 seconds total)

**Check 1: Golden Copy Integrity (target: 5s)**
```rust
fn check_golden_copies() -> Result<()> {
    let golden_dir = Path::new("sample_sqlite_db_files/golden");
    let checksums = load_checksums("sample_sqlite_db_files/checksums.sha256")?;

    assert!(!checksums.is_empty(), "No golden copies found");

    for (filename, expected_hash) in &checksums {
        let path = golden_dir.join(filename);
        assert!(path.exists(), "Missing golden copy: {filename}");
        let actual_hash = sha256_file(&path)?;
        assert_eq!(&actual_hash, expected_hash, "Hash mismatch for {filename}");
    }
    info!(count = checksums.len(), "Golden copies verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

**Check 2: FrankenSQLite Can Open + Query (target: 5s)**
```rust
fn check_frankensqlite() -> Result<()> {
    let conn = fsqlite::Connection::open(":memory:")?;
    conn.execute("CREATE TABLE smoke_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, val TEXT)")?;
    conn.execute("INSERT INTO smoke_test VALUES (1, 'hello')")?;
    let rows = conn.query("SELECT * FROM smoke_test")?;
    assert_eq!(rows.len(), 1);
    info!("FrankenSQLite: CREATE/INSERT/SELECT verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

**Check 3: rusqlite (C SQLite) Can Open + Query (target: 5s)**
```rust
fn check_csqlite() -> Result<()> {
    let conn = rusqlite::Connection::open_in_memory()?;
    conn.execute("CREATE TABLE smoke_test (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, val TEXT)", [])?;
    conn.execute("INSERT INTO smoke_test VALUES (1, 'hello')", [])?;
    let count: i64 = conn.query_row("SELECT count(*) FROM smoke_test", [], |r| r.get(0))?;
    assert_eq!(count, 1);
    info!("C SQLite (rusqlite): CREATE/INSERT/SELECT verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

**Check 4: Workload Generator Determinism (target: 3s)**
```rust
fn check_workload_determinism() -> Result<()> {
    let config = WorkloadConfig { seed: 42, num_operations: 100, ..default() };
    let run1 = WorkloadGenerator::new(config.clone()).generate();
    let run2 = WorkloadGenerator::new(config).generate();
    assert_eq!(run1, run2, "Workload generator not deterministic!");
    info!(operations = 100, "Workload determinism verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

**Check 5: Corruption Injector + Detection (target: 5s)**
```rust
fn check_corruption_injector() -> Result<()> {
    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
    // Create a temp database
    let db_path = tmp.path().join("test.db");
    let conn = rusqlite::Connection::open(&db_path)?;
    conn.execute("CREATE TABLE t (id INTEGER)", [])?;
    conn.execute("INSERT INTO t VALUES (1)", [])?;
    drop(conn);

    let before_hash = sha256_file(&db_path)?;
    let injector = CorruptionInjector::new(db_path.clone());
    let report = injector.inject(&CorruptionPattern::BitFlip { byte_offset: 100, bit_position: 0 })?;
    let after_hash = sha256_file(&db_path)?;

    assert_ne!(before_hash, after_hash, "Corruption didn't change file");
    assert_eq!(report.affected_bytes, 1);
    info!("Corruption injection + detection verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

**Check 6: Canonicalization Pipeline (target: 5s)**
```rust
fn check_canonicalization() -> Result<()> {
    // Create two databases with same content, different insert order
    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
    let db1 = create_test_db(tmp.path(), "db1.db", &[1, 2, 3])?;
    let db2 = create_test_db(tmp.path(), "db2.db", &[3, 1, 2])?;

    let result = canonicalize_and_compare(&db1, &db2)?;
    assert!(result.logical_match, "Same data should match logically");
    info!("Canonicalization pipeline verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

**Check 7: Logging Pipeline (target: 2s)**
```rust
fn check_logging() -> Result<()> {
    // Verify JSON log file is created and parseable
    let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new()?;
    init_logging(tmp.path(), false)?;
    info!("smoke_test_event");
    // Flush and verify log file contains the event
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(tmp.path().join("test.log.jsonl"))?;
    assert!(log_content.contains("smoke_test_event"));
    info!("Logging pipeline verified");
    Ok(())
}
```

### Output
```
$ e2e-runner run-smoke
[SMOKE] Golden Copy Integrity.............. OK (3.2s) — 20 files verified
[SMOKE] FrankenSQLite Backend.............. OK (0.8s) — CREATE/INSERT/SELECT
[SMOKE] C SQLite (rusqlite) Backend........ OK (0.4s) — CREATE/INSERT/SELECT
[SMOKE] Workload Generator Determinism..... OK (0.3s) — 100 ops, 2 runs identical
[SMOKE] Corruption Injector................ OK (1.1s) — BitFlip injected + detected
[SMOKE] Canonicalization Pipeline.......... OK (2.4s) — Tier 2 logical match
[SMOKE] Logging Pipeline................... OK (0.2s) — JSONL output verified

All 7 checks passed in 8.4s. Infrastructure ready for full E2E suite.
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- All 7 checks run in <30s total
- Each check has clear pass/fail output with timing
- First failure aborts with clear error message
- Exit code 0 if all pass, 1 if any fail
- Can run as a CI gate before full suite
- 7 unit tests (one per check) plus integration test for full smoke run
","created_at":"2026-02-10T05:04:37Z"},{"id":1738,"issue_id":"bd-dcjy","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## bd-dcjy: Fast Smoke Test — Implementation Complete

### What was implemented

Created `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/smoke.rs` — a 30-second infrastructure verification module with 7 checks:

1. **Golden Copy Integrity** — Validates all `.db` files in `golden/` via `PRAGMA integrity_check`. Gracefully skips if directory doesn't exist (CI/fresh checkouts).
2. **FrankenSQLite Backend** — In-memory `CREATE TABLE` / `INSERT` / `SELECT` via `fsqlite::Connection`.
3. **C SQLite (rusqlite) Backend** — In-memory `CREATE TABLE` / `INSERT` / `SELECT count(*)` via `rusqlite`.
4. **Workload Generator Determinism** — Generates two OpLogs from same seed (42), verifies JSON serialization is identical.
5. **Corruption Injector** — Creates a test DB, hashes before, injects a BitFlip, hashes after, verifies change.
6. **Canonicalization Pipeline** — Runs `canonical_sha256` twice on same DB, verifies stable hash.
7. **Logging Pipeline** — Creates scoped JSON subscriber, emits a test event, verifies valid JSON output.

### Public API

- `run_smoke_tests() -> SmokeTestReport` — runs all 7 checks sequentially
- `format_smoke_report(&SmokeTestReport) -> String` — human-readable summary
- `SmokeCheckResult` / `SmokeTestReport` — serializable result types

### Tests (8 passing)

Individual check tests + full suite structure test + serialization test. Full suite completes in ~0.15s (well under 30s target).

### Quality gates

- `cargo check` — clean
- `cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings` — clean (pedantic + nursery)
- `cargo fmt` — clean
- All 245 lib tests passing (including 8 new)

Agent: AmberFinch

 {"id":"bd-dkig","title":"MemoryVfs: remove SQLite-style lock escalation (no serialized writers)","description":"## Problem

`crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/memory.rs` implemented SQLite-style file lock escalation (Shared -> Reserved -> Pending -> Exclusive) and returned Busy based on other handles.

That reintroduces file-level writer serialization, which violates FrankenSQLite's core design: concurrent writers are coordinated via MVCC at the page level, not by SQLite's file-lock protocol. In this project, MemoryVfs file locking should be a minimal stub.

## Fix

- Make `MemoryFile::lock` / `MemoryFile::unlock` minimal stubs (local bookkeeping only; no cross-handle coordination; never Busy).
- Make `check_reserved_lock` always return false for MemoryVfs.
- Update/replace any tests that assumed SQLite-style contention.

## Done When

- MemoryVfs lock/unlock no longer blocks other writers at the file level.
- `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --check` are green.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:29:52.983887641Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:36:01.737866633Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:36:01.737836767Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: lock/unlock are minimal stubs (local bookkeeping only, never Busy), check_reserved_lock returns false. test_locking_contention verifies both handles take Exclusive without blocking. All 48 memory VFS tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","vfs"]}

## Unit Test Requirements
- test_windows_ci_unit_suite: All WindowsVfs unit tests pass on Windows.
- test_windows_ci_lock_byte: Lock-byte range operations verified on NTFS.
- test_windows_ci_file_mapping: CreateFileMapping SHM operations verified.

## E2E Test

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: emit Win32 API call parameters and return codes.
- INFO: emit test suite results and interop comparison summary.
- WARN: emit platform-specific behavior differences.
- ERROR: emit Win32 API failures with GetLastError codes.
